free_text: Fission and spallation competition from the
intermediate nuclei americium-241 and neptunium-235
U233 data.
Berkeley 60-inch cyclotron.
Windowless proportional counter
(Nucleometer) for spallation. The fission products,
all of which were beta minus emitters, were counted on
a standard Geiger-Mueller counter.
Due to the large number of possible nuclear
reactions induced in the target nuclei and in the
aluminum backing plate, extensive chemical procedures
were necessary to purify the products for which
yields were to be determined. The chemical yields of
the various products were determined by the addition
of standardized weights of carriers for each of the
fission products and known disintegration rates of
alpha-emitting tracers for the spallation products.
The disintegration rate, hence the number of
atoms, of a given product nuclide was determined by
resolution of decay curves or when possible by
resolving radiations of a given unique energy. Four
types of counters were used, the first three being
used to determine the disintegration rates or the
yields for the spallation products, the latter being
used for fission product determinations.
BP
The "low GT" Pu239 contained only 0.02 atom percent
of Pu240 and was essentially free of Pu241.
The limits of error are estimated
to be about 10-20%.

The total fission cross section was obtained by summing
the individual fission yield curves over the mass
numbers from A=70 to A=170.
The cross sections for 20.2 and 20.6 MeV deuterons
have been averaged and plotted for 20.4 MeV deuterons
since these two bombardments fall within the limits
of error of the energy determination of being at the
same energy.
No details on sources of uncertainties.

With neptunium, considerable difficulty was
encountered in obtaining acceptable target plates.
Frequently, targets containing only 0.06 to 0.1
mg/cm2 of neptunium were used although on occasion up
to 0.4 mg/cm2 could be plated successfully.
The limits of error are estimated
to be about 10-20%.
Corrected cross section for mass chain.
